perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Bank will use their best efforts to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be declared effective by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the Form AC or the Holding Company Application to be approved by the OTS and will immediately upon receipt of any information concerning the events listed below notify the Agent:
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Bank will endeavor to cause any post-effective amendment to the Registration Statement to be declared effective by the Commission and any post-effective amendment to the Form AC or the Holding Company Application to be approved by the OTS and will attempt to notify the Agent upon receipt of information concerning certain events.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'use their best efforts' to 'endeavor' and 'immediately notify' to 'attempt to notify' introduces ambiguity regarding the level of commitment and timeliness required. This weakens the obligation to ensure regulatory compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b-5 prohibits omitting material facts necessary to make statements not misleading. By weakening the notification requirement, the modified text increases the risk of failing to promptly disclose information that could affect investment decisions, potentially violating this rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity into the contract by changing "use their best efforts" to "endeavor" and "immediately notify" to "attempt to notify." This creates a weaker obligation regarding regulatory compliance and information disclosure. According to Rule 10b-5, it is unlawful to omit material facts necessary to make statements not misleading. By weakening the notification requirement, the modified text increases the risk of failing to promptly disclose information that could affect investment decisions. The original contract ensured greater certainty and diligence in adhering to regulatory requirements, while the revised language introduces an unacceptable level of ambiguity concerning the company's commitment to providing timely and accurate information, and, therefore, the change does create a legal contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that it is unlawful "to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading."

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Bank will comply with any and all material terms, conditions, requirements and provisions with respect to the Offering imposed by the Commission, the OTS or the Conversion Regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1933 Act Regulations, the 1934 Act and the 1934 Act Regulations to be complied with prior to or subsequent to the Closing Date and when the Prospectus is required to be delivered, and during such time period the Company and the Bank will comply, at their own expense, with all material requirements imposed upon them by the Commission, the OTS or the Conversion Regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1933 Act Regulations, the 1934 Act and the 1934 Act Regulations, including, without limitation, Rule 10b-5 under the 1934 Act, in each case as from time to time in force, so far as necessary to permit the continuance of sales or dealing in the Common Shares during such period in accordance with the provisions hereof and the Prospectus.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Bank will aim to comply with the material terms, conditions, requirements and provisions with respect to the Offering imposed by the Commission, the OTS or the Conversion Regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1933 Act Regulations, the 1934 Act and the 1934 Act Regulations to be complied with prior to or subsequent to the Closing Date. The Company and the Bank will attempt to comply, at their own expense, with the requirements imposed upon them by the Commission, the OTS or the Conversion Regulations, and by the 1933 Act, the 1933 Act Regulations, the 1934 Act and the 1934 Act Regulations, including, without limitation, Rule 10b-5 under the 1934 Act, as necessary to permit the continuance of sales or dealing in the Common Shares during such period in accordance with the provisions hereof and the Prospectus.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'will comply' with 'will aim to comply' and 'will attempt to comply' introduces a level of discretion and uncertainty regarding the commitment to full compliance with securities laws, undermining the original stringent obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933 requires registration of securities before offering or sale. By reducing the obligation to 'aim to comply,' the modified text creates a risk that securities may be offered or sold without proper registration or compliance, violating Section 5.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 15 / CHAPTER 2A / SUBCHAPTER I / § 77e Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 15 USC 77e : Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 15-COMMERCE AND TRADE CHAPTER 2A-SECURITIES AND TRUST INDENTURES SUBCHAPTER I-DOMESTIC SECURITIES Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Amendments Effective Date §77e. Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails (a) Sale or delivery after sale of unregistered securities Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly- (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to comply with securities laws by changing 'will comply' to 'aim to comply' and 'attempt to comply.' This introduces ambiguity and discretion, potentially leading to non-compliance. Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933, as referenced in the Law Explanation, requires registration of securities before offering or sale. The changed text creates a risk that securities may be offered or sold without proper compliance, undermining the legal requirement for full compliance. Scraped Law Snippet 1, citing 15 USC 77e, discusses 'Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails' and makes it 'unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly...to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce' unless a registration statement is in effect. This snippet underscores the necessity for strict adherence to securities regulations, which is compromised by the ambiguity introduced in the modified contract.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will make generally available to its security holders as soon as practicable, but not later than 60 days after the close of the period covered thereby, an earnings statement (in form complying with the provisions of Rule 158 of the 1933 Act Regulations) covering a twelve-month period beginning not later than the first day of the Company's fiscal quarter next following the effective date (as defined in such Rule 158) of the Registration Statement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will endeavor to make available to its security holders an earnings statement covering a twelve-month period beginning around the first day of the Company's fiscal quarter next following the effective date of the Registration Statement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the deadline ('not later than 60 days') and the compliance requirement with Rule 158, and changing 'as soon as practicable' to 'endeavor to make available' introduces significant ambiguity about when and how the earnings statement will be provided. Replacing 'the first day' with 'around the first day' further adds to the ambiguity.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 13a-13 requires companies to file quarterly reports on Form 10-Q, including specified financial information, within a set timeframe. The modified text's removal of deadlines and specific compliance requirements weakens the obligation to provide timely financial information, potentially violating Rule 13a-13.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13a-13 - Quarterly reports on Form 10-Q (§ 249.308a of this chapter). | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13a-13 - Quarterly reports on Form 10-Q (§ 249.308a of this chapter). CFR prev | next § 240.13a-13 Quarterly reports on Form 10-Q (§ 249.308a of this chapter). (a) Except as provided in paragraphs (b) and (c) of this section , every issuer that has securities registered pursuant to section 12 of the Act and is required to file annual reports pursuant to section 13 of the Act, and has filed or intends to file such reports on Form 10-K ( § 249.310 of this chapter), shall file a quarterly report on Form 10-Q (§ 249.308a of this chapter) within the period specified in General Instruction A.1. to that form for each of the first three quarters of each fiscal year of the issuer , commencing with the first fiscal quarter following the most recent fiscal year for which full financial statements were included in the registration statement , or, if the registration statement included financial statements for an interim period subsequent to the most recent fiscal year end meeting the requirements of Article 10 of Regulation S-X and Rule 8-03 of Regulation S-X for smaller reporting companies, for the first fiscal quarter subsequent to the quarter reported upon in the registration statement . The first quarterly report of the issuer shall be filed either within 45 days after the effective date of the registration statement or on or before the date on which such report would have been required to be filed if the issuer has been required to file reports on Form 10-Q as of its last fiscal quarter, whichever is later. (b) The provisions of this rule shall not apply to the following issuers: (1) Investment companies required to file reports pursuant to § 270.30a-1 ; (2) Foreign private issuers required to file reports pursuant to § 240.13a-16 ; and (3) Asset-backed issu
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts Rule 13a-13 by removing specific deadlines and compliance requirements for filing quarterly reports. The original text mandated an earnings statement be available within 60 days, complying with Rule 158. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the company will 'endeavor to make available' an earnings statement 'around the first day' without any deadline. Snippet 2, referencing 17 CFR § 240.13a-13, requires issuers to file a quarterly report on Form 10-Q within a period specified in General Instruction A.1. By removing concrete deadlines and compliance with Rule 158, the changed text weakens the obligation for timely financial reporting, potentially violating Rule 13a-13, thus creating a legal contradiction.
